rulings.Case Title: CURTIS, MARTHA INDIVIDUALLY ON HER OWN BEHALF AND VS. GIELING, IMOGENE B. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 03, 2016 line 1. PLAINTIFF CURTIS, MARTHA INDIVIDUALLY ON HER OWN BEHALF's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. To the extent Plaintiff move s for summary judgment (Notice of Motion 2:1-2), the motion is denied as it does not dispose of the whole complaint. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied as Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: DINAKER JUVVADI VS. SAVANNAH CULBERSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 03, 2016 line 4. DEFENDANT HENRY VARGAS, SAVANNAH CULBERTSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend to allege compliance with or exemption from SFRO. Defendant and her counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: BERTHA LUZ ALFARO VS. CYBERNIB CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 03, 2016 line 5. DEFENDANT SAVANNAH CULBERTSON, HENRY VARGAS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges an unlawful detainer based on breach of a commercial lease (attached to complaint). Defendants and their counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: ANSELMO SAUCEDO ET AL VS. BISMARCK NUNEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 03, 2016 line 6. DEFENDANT BISMARCK NUNEZ, SANDY FLORES' AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. This demurrer fails to address all the grounds on which this action is based. "Ordinarily, a general demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action and if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841. (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz
